Family Trichonotidae
sand-divers                                                                                                                 Family Uranoscopidae
stargazers

cj

ecp

Ichthyscopus lebeck
Longnosed stargazer

in red are those listed among the threatened animals of Singapore
from Ng, P. K. L. & Y. C. Wee, 1994. The Singapore Red Data Book: Threatened Plants and Animals of Singapore.

Location in italics indicates sightings shared by others.

Mangroves

sbw: sungei buloh wetland reserve including kranji mangroves, lck: lim chu kang

Northern shores

ecp: east coast park, tm: tanah merah, prp:pasir ris park,chg: changi, ubn: pulau ubin (other than cj)cj: chek jawa, skd: pulau sekudu, bb: beting bronok, tus: tuas

Southern shores

lab: labrador + berlayar creek, snt: sentosa, cyr: cyrene reef, kus: kusu island, laz: lazarus island, sji: st. john’s island, sis: sisters islands, jng: pulau jong, tkr:pulau tekukor, hnt: pulau hantu, tpg: terumbus pempang darat, tengah and laut, smk: pulau semakau, tsm: submerged reefs off semakau - terumbu raya, beting bemban besar, terumbu bemban, terumbu semakau, rfl: raffles lighthouse, lfa: live firing area (Salu, Sudong, Pawai, Senang, Biola and submerged reefs in the area)

About citing this checklist.

photo index of
fishes on this site

all vertebrates

all fishes

typical fish-shape
  small < 5cm
medium 5-10cm
large > 10cm

globular
boxy, stone-like

flattened fishes
flattened downwards
  sting rays
others

disk-shaped
flattened sideways

long

  eel-, worm-like
stick-like

others

  mudskippers
odd shaped
living w others

links | references | about | email Ria

Spot errors? Have a question? Want to share your sightings? email Ria I’ll be glad to hear from you! wildfactsheets website©ria tan 2008
